question: In John 8:58, why is the wording ‘Before Abraham was, I AM’ more explosive than it sounds in English—and what OT text does it collide with in Jewish hearing?

choices.A: It echoes the divine ‘I AM’ language associated with God’s self-identification, colliding with Exodus 3:14 and Isaianic ‘I am he’ claims.
choices.B: It only means Jesus is older than Abraham by a few years.
choices.C: It is a grammar mistake and should be ignored.
choices.D: It means Abraham existed after Jesus in history.

teaching.title: ‘I AM’ as identity-claim
teaching.body: John frames Jesus using ‘I AM’ in a context where his opponents react violently. The force isn’t merely pre-existence; it’s the resonance with God’s self-identification and unique divine identity language in Israel’s scriptures. That’s why it lands as scandal, not trivia.
